page_info.summary: Activated sludge is a biological wastewater treatment process used in municipal and industrial facilities, where microorganisms consume organic pollutants in an aerated environment, maintaining a balance between food, biomass, and oxygen. Common operational problems include poor settling, bulking sludge (SVI above 150 mL/g due to filamentous bacteria, low dissolved oxygen (DO below 2 mg/L), low F/M ratio, old sludge (SRT above 15 days)), foaming (Nocardia, Microthrix), nitrification failure (high ammonia, low SRT, low DO, low alkalinity, cold temperature), low dissolved oxygen (DO below 2 mg/L due to inadequate aeration or organic overloading), and high energy consumption (aeration accounts for 50-90% of total WWTP energy, often from over-aeration or inefficient diffusers). Root causes include operational stress (improper F/M ratio 0.2-0.5, SRT 3-15 days), influent variability (temperature, toxic shock from heavy metals, organic compounds, inorganic compounds), and equipment degradation (diffuser fouling, blower problems, RAS pump failures). Troubleshooting involves systematic identification of the exact problem, confirming the root cause (biological, operational, mechanical, influent-related), and applying targeted fixes such as adjusting sludge wasting, increasing DO, cleaning diffusers, or repairing equipment. The decision to fix versus replace components, like aeration equipment (diffusers, blowers, mechanical aerators) or pumps, considers equipment age, repair costs, and potential energy savings, where automated DO control systems reduce energy by 10-30% and modern jet aeration systems from Mixing Systems can cut energy by up to 40%. Preventive measures include routine monitoring (DO, MLSS, SVI, microscopic examinations for stalked ciliates and rotifers), proactive maintenance, and optimizing process control for aeration, sludge wasting, and sludge recirculation to maintain stable performance and meet permit limits.

page_info.interlinking_description: Comprehensive troubleshooting guide for activated sludge wastewater treatment covering bulking, foaming, poor settling, and effluent quality issues. Addresses root causes including dissolved oxygen levels, F/M ratio imbalances, filamentous bacteria growth, and nutrient deficiencies. Includes corrective actions, component repair vs replacement decisions, and preventive maintenance strategies for municipal and industrial treatment plants.
